title: Daily Briefing — Apr 13
headline: Market coiling under the surface as leadership quietly rotates — don't be fooled by the calm.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: S&P (SPY $679.46) is nudging above its 20MA ($657.72) with tech and megacaps holding the line (NVDA, AAPL, AMZN all above their 20MAs). VIX remains elevated at 21.25 but trending down – volatility compression after a fear spike, not a fresh panic. Fear & Greed is 37.7 — recovering from last week's extreme fear but we're NOT in full FOMO mode yet.

Rotation is the story: Industrials (XLI) and Utilities (XLU, XLI $171.52, XLU $46.96) are leading while Energy (XLE $56.94) and Materials soften. Tech (XLK $142.62) is back in 'improving' territory on the RRG — watch for fresh leadership if these moves confirm above the 20MA. Real Estate (XLRE $42.82) triggered higher and is among the relative strength winners — not what you'd expect if recession was imminent.

Contrarian signal? Retail is NOT piling in, breadth is stable, and smart money isn't running for the hills. But XLF (Financials) is lagging and below its 200MA ($52.28) — the canary isn't singing but it's not exactly chirpy either. No gamma wall breakdowns or margin debt warnings yet, but keep an eye on Financials for early cracks.
callout_body:
SPY: $657.72 = 20MA, bulls in control above
XLK: $138.60 = 200MA, base in play if held
XLF: $50.82 = 50MA, $52.28 = 200MA, watch for support test
VIX: Below 24.7 = volatility contraction, not crisis
NVDA: $177.42 = 20MA, leading tech barometer
XLE: $56.26 = 50MA, slipping below is a risk-off tell
action_items: Watch Financials (XLF) — reduction in equity exposure if $50.77 loses 200MA ($52.28), per rule R010.
Look for tech confirmation: NVDA, AAPL, and AMZN all need continued closes above 20MA for bullish continuation — don't chase, let confirmation come.
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict — less trades, more wins.
